title: How to override tls certificate path ?
body:
Hello,

when I want to copy a docker image into my private registry using tls skopeo is using `/etc/docker/certs.d/container-registry.lab.stack:5000` as path to find my ca, cert and key.
However I do not have this path because I use cri-o instead of docker.

I would like for skopeo to have a look into `/etc/containers/certs.d/container-registry.lab.stack:5000`

By default it should look into `/etc/containers/certs.d/container-registry.lab.stack:5000`

I was unable to find the documentation about this change.

How can I override it ?

```
[root@kube-cp1 certs.d]# skopeo copy docker://registry.k8s.io/kube-apiserver:v1.26.3 docker://container-registry.lab.stack:5000/kube-apiserver:v1.26.3 --dest-tls-verify=true --dest-creds labstack:labstack --debug
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d 
DEBU[0000] Returning credentials for container-registry.lab.stack:5000/kube-apiserver from DockerAuthConfig 
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for container-registry.lab.stack:5000/kube-apiserver:v1.26.3, using built-in default file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore 
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/container-registry.lab.stack:5000 
```

I have followed this link to create my private repository successfully https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docs/containers-certs.d.5.md

I am able to copy an image defining the `--dest-cert-dir` however I do not want to specify it each time I need to do a copy.

Stack: Fedora-Cloud-Base-37-1.7.x86_64.qcow2 
cri-o:1.24

Thanks by advance,

Regards,

Damien

comments.body: My bad ... it is working ... sorry for opening this issue :(
